name: Rogan Josh- Madhur Jaffrey
recipeIngredient: 2 1-inch chunks fresh ginger, peeled, coarsely chopped
2 pounds boned lamb shoulder or leg, cut into 1-inch cubes
1 teaspoon cayenne pepper, ground (adjust to taste)
1 dash fresh ground pepper to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Put the ginger, garlic, and 4 tablespoons of water into an electric blender. Blend well into a smooth paste.
2. Heat oil in a wide, heavy pot over medium-high flame. Brown meat cubes in several batches and set aside in a bowl.
3. Add cardamom, bay leaves, cloves, peppercorns, and cinnamon to the hot oil. Stir once and wait until cloves swell and the bay leaves begin to take on color (a few seconds).
4. Put in the onions. Fry for 5 minutes or until onions turn medium-brown.
5. Add ginger-garlic paste and stir for 30 seconds.
6. Incorporate coriander, cumin, paprika, cayenne, and salt. Stir fry for another 30 seconds.
7. Add browned meat cubes and the meat juices. Pour in 1 tablespoon of yogurt and stir until well blended (about 30 seconds).
8. Add remaining yogurt, a tablespoon at a time, stirring and blending until desired consistency is reached.
9. Add 1 1/4 cups water, bring to boil, scrape browned spices from pot sides and bottom, then cover and simmer on low heat for about an hour or until meat is tender.
10. Every 10 minutes, stir the pot to prevent burning.
11. When meat is tender, remove lid, turn heat to medium-high, and reduce liquid by boiling off until sauce thickens (stir continuously).
12. Sprinkle garam masala and fresh ground pepper over the dish just before serving.
